summary: Moved most of the common fighter DamageIce logic out of asm into a new `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageIce.c`/`.h`, wired it into the build, and introduced supporting type/header cleanup needed to express ice damage motion variables, collision-box geometry, common-data offsets, and fighter attribute names. The PR report shows full matches for most smaller DamageIce callbacks/helpers, while `ftCo_80090B60` and `ftCo_80091030` remained partial. There were no review comments in the slice.
what_changed: Added `ftCo_DamageIce.c` with C implementations or guarded matching-asm fallbacks for DamageIce-related functions including `ftCo_80090984`, `ftCo_800909D0`, `ftCo_80090AC0`, `ftCo_80090B48`, `ftCo_80090B60`, `ftCo_80091030`, `ftCo_80091274`, and the `ftCo_DamageIce_*` callbacks.
Deleted the bulk of the corresponding assembly from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s`; the changed-file summary shows 799 deleted asm lines and only 18 added there.
Added `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_DamageIce.h` with prototypes, extern constants, and an `M2CTX` stack layout for `ftCo_80090B60`.
Added `ftCo_DamageIce.c.o` to `obj_files.mk`, making the new C translation unit part of the build.
Extended common fighter motion variables with `mv.co.damageice`, including byte fields at `fp+2340..2343`, a float at `fp+2344`, and an `ftCollisionBox` at `fp+2348`.
Introduced temporary `src/melee/ft/ftcolltypes.h` defining `struct ftCollisionBox { float top; float bottom; Vec2 left; Vec2 right; }` and used it from common fighter types.

Refined `ftCommonData` around offsets `0x778..0x7A0`, replacing a filler block with typed fields such as `x788`, `x78C`, `x790`, `x794`, `x798`, `x79C`, and float `x7A0`, which DamageIce uses for timers/effect scaling.

Renamed many `ftCo_DatAttrs` fields from offset-heavy names to semantic snake_case names such as `grounded_max_horizontal_velocity`, `max_jumps`, `aerial_friction`, `fast_fall_velocity`, `bubble_ratio`, and others; many fighter files were updated to use the new names.
Updated `ftcoll.h` to include `lb/forward.h` and type `ftColl_8007B5AC` with `HurtCapsule*` for its second parameter.
Renamed assembly label `.L_800886D8` to global symbol `ftCo_800886D8` in `asm/melee/ft/ft_0877.s` and added related declarations.
Changed `tools/report_score.py` sorting so report entries sort by percent ascending, then max score descending, then name ascending.
smart_moves: Kept partially solved hard functions isolated behind `#if defined(MUST_MATCH) && !defined(WIP)` assembly fallbacks while providing C/non-matching bodies or `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` stubs, allowing incremental decomp progress without pretending the large routines were fully solved.
Used an `M2CTX` stack struct in the header for `ftCo_80090B60`, preserving inferred stack-slot knowledge for a large matrix/JObj-heavy routine and making future matching attempts searchable.

Factored the ice bubble geometry into `ftCollisionBox` and `mv.co.damageice` fields instead of leaving raw offsets like `0x2348`, improving later readability around top/bottom/left/right capsule or box bounds.

Pulled common attribute names into semantic snake_case across the repo, reducing future decomp friction when DamageIce and unrelated fighter routines need values like jump counts, aerial friction, grounded max velocity, or bubble ratio.

Introduced small helper structure around effect spawning (`efAsync_Spawn` with effect id `1045`/`0x415`) and used typed `ftParts_8007500C` part lookups, making the ice visual-effect setup easier to reason about.
Improved `report_score.py` tie-breaking so post-PR matching reports are more stable and prioritize large remaining score targets when percentages tie.
decomp_lessons: For large fighter-state routines, moving the file to C can still be useful even when the biggest functions are partial: this PR fully matched many small callbacks while preserving asm for the remaining hard sections.

DamageIce uses several fighter subsystems at once: motion-state changes, common damage setup, velocity transfer depending on ground/air state, effect spawning, collision/hurtbox setup, matrix/JObj transforms, and callback installation.

Naming struct fields used broadly by many fighters may be necessary before a localized match is maintainable; this PR updated many unrelated character files because `ftCo_DatAttrs` names changed globally.
When a motion var overlays a common union at known offsets, define a named sub-struct in `ftCommon/types.h` so later code can replace offset stores with meaningful fields.

Temporary headers can be acceptable when they isolate newly discovered types, but the file-level `@todo This is temporary` in `ftcolltypes.h` signals future cleanup should consolidate collision type definitions.
Typed prototypes matter for matching and correctness: changing `ftColl_8007B5AC` from `void*` to `HurtCapsule*` captures an inferred argument role and helps call sites compile with more structure.
naming_conventions: Common fighter functions in this area use the `ftCo_` prefix, with address-based names retained when semantics are unknown, e.g. `ftCo_80090984`, `ftCo_80090B60`, and `ftCo_80091030`.
Action-state callbacks use descriptive suffixes such as `ftCo_DamageIce_Anim`, `ftCo_DamageIce_IASA`, `ftCo_DamageIce_Phys`, and `ftCo_DamageIce_Coll`.
Common motion variables are nested under `fp->mv.co.damageice` with short offset-preserving field names (`x0`, `x4`, `x8`) when exact semantic names are not yet proven.
Fighter common-data offsets remain named as `x788`, `x78C`, `x7A0` when semantics are still unknown, but typed as floats or `UNK_T` instead of hidden filler.

Fighter attribute fields were converted from mixed offset/descriptive names like `x144_GroundedMaxHorizontalVelocity` and `x168_MaxJumps` to lower snake_case names such as `grounded_max_horizontal_velocity` and `max_jumps`.
The new temporary collision type is named `ftCollisionBox`, with plain geometric field names `top`, `bottom`, `left`, and `right`.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Removed matched functions from `asm/melee/ft/ft_08A4.s` while adding C equivalents in `ftCo_DamageIce.c`.
Used `#ifdef MWERKS_GEKKO` constants for floats/doubles such as `ftCo_804D8518`, `ftCo_804D851C`, `ftCo_804D8520`, `ftCo_804D8528`, and `ftCo_804D8530` to mirror small-data constants.
Used guarded inline assembly for must-match builds on difficult functions, especially `ftCo_80090B60` and `ftCo_80091030`, rather than forcing inaccurate C.
Added explicit unused-stack padding under `MUST_MATCH` in functions such as `ftCo_800909D0` and the effect helper, indicating stack-shape preservation was part of the matching strategy.
Preserved inferred stack layouts with an `M2CTX` struct containing matrices, quaternion, Vec3 translation, and stack locals for `ftCo_80090B60`.

The PR report indicates `ftCo_DamageIce_Anim`, `ftCo_800909D0`, `ftCo_80090AC0`, `ftCo_DamageIce_Phys`, `ftCo_DamageIce_Coll`, `ftCo_80090984`, `ftCo_80091274`, `ftCo_80090B48`, and `ftCo_DamageIce_IASA` reached 100%, while `ftCo_80090B60` and `ftCo_80091030` remained partial.
Changed a local assembly label to a global symbol (`ftCo_800886D8`) to make cross-file references and declarations possible.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that finish matching `ftCo_80090B60` or replace its `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` body.
Find later PRs that finish matching `ftCo_80091030` or remove its guarded assembly fallback.
Find cleanup PRs that move `ftCollisionBox` out of temporary `ftcolltypes.h` or rename its fields.
Find PRs that assign semantic names to `p_ftCommonData->x788`, `x78C`, `x790`, and `x7A0` used by DamageIce.
Find review or follow-up discussion around the broad `ftCo_DatAttrs` rename from offset-style names to snake_case.
